summary.zh: This patch fixes packaged Miko releases that could fail to start because a required Pi SDK module was missing.
summary.en: This patch fixes packaged Miko releases that could fail to start because a required Pi SDK module was missing.

title.en: Package the Pi SDK runtime

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only ID English only Codex English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only image-gen English onlyEnglish only
summary.en: This release hardens startup and upgrades: the update progress bar now runs continuously across both server and renderer downloads; session event IDs are no longer mistaken for Codex model IDs in the catalog; failed or incomplete migrations no longer halt the whole chain, and source bytes are preserved for retry; corrupt early-epoch metadata no longer blocks launch. Media generation runtime moves into core, with a display change for sessions that used the retired image-gen plugin.

title.en: Continuous update progress across both artifacts

summary.zh: English only 0–100 English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Server and renderer downloads share one continuous 0–100 progress bar, and the verify gap no longer flashes an applying state.

title.en: Keep startup available across partial migration failures

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: One failed migration no longer stops the rest of the chain; failed items retry on the next launch.

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only

It also removes obsolete direct-message tool guidance from channels.

title.en: Data-directory epoch blocks older kernels

summary.zh: English only epoch English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only epoch English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Large downloads abort and rotate mirrors after prolonged near-zero progress or a hard per-attempt timeout. In-process pointer writes are serialized so crash-retry and activation can no longer erase a confirmed update.

fix(update): English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only

title.en: Only one kernel may own the same data directory

summary.zh: English only token English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Startup probes any recorded kernel with token auth. If it is still alive, launch is refused with guidance to quit the other process; if not, stale lock files are cleaned automatically so two processes do not share one data directory.

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyMac English only JIT English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only Agent English onlyEnglish only
summary.en: This release makes updates more reliable and visible: macOS hot updates no longer crash the server at launch due to missing JIT entitlements; update checks compare the origin and mirror in parallel and reject channel-mismatched manifests; older shelf content is no longer offered as an update; crash fallbacks surface a clear notice; and memory reminders only reach sessions owned by the source Agent.

title.en: Clear notice after a crash fallback

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: When repeated launch failures trigger an automatic rollback, the sidebar shows a one-time notice explaining which version failed, which version was restored, and that the bad version is quarantined from automatic retry.

title.en: Fix macOS hot-update server crashing on launch

summary.zh: English only Node English only JIT English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: The bundled Node binary now receives JIT entitlements during Developer ID signing, with a pre-pack launch smoke test so a missing executable-memory entitlement can no longer crash the server right after a hot update.

sources.title: fix(build): macOS server English only node English only JIT entitlement English only

title.en: Dual-source update manifests with channel checks

summary.zh: GitHub update manifest checks are verified before use.
summary.en: Update checks query GitHub, verify manifest signatures, and reject manifests whose declared channel does not match the requested channel.

sources.title: fix(update): English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only

title.en: Older shelf content is no longer treated as an update

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: If shelf content is older than the locally active version, both check and download now treat the install as current, preventing an older train from being offered as an update and applied as a downgrade.

sources.title: fix(update): English only

title.en: Memory reminders stay on the owning Agent

summary.zh: English only Agent English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only Agent English onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Memory fact changes are now injected only into sessions owned by the source Agent, instead of being broadcast to every Agent's sessions.

sources.title: fix(reminder): scope memory facts to owning agent

summary.zh: Miko English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Miko now evaluates component updates more accurately: same-version content is no longer reported as a new update, while components that require a newer app shell are blocked before download with a clear upgrade prompt. This release also uses one shared renderer update archive across all four platforms to prevent same-version content drift.

title.en: Same-version content no longer appears as an update

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyMiko English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: When the active local content already matches the update train version, Miko now treats it as current instead of offering a duplicate update that cannot be applied. All four platforms also reuse the same renderer archive, preventing build-time metadata from producing different hashes for the same version.

sources.title: fix(update): English only"English only"
feat(build): renderer English only CI English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only

title.en: Incompatible component updates are blocked early

summary.zh: English only API English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only“English only”English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Component updates now enforce their required interface API and server protocol versions. If the installed app shell is too old, both update checks and downloads stop with the existing clear prompt to update the app itself, avoiding a failure after download.

sources.title: feat(update): English only

summary.zh: Miko English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only Windows English onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Miko's component updates are now fully user-initiated: background checks no longer download or modify local files, and users can start an available update from the update card or About page while following its progress. This release also protects the active version directory and improves Windows scrollbars.

title.en: Users decide when component updates begin

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Background and manual checks now only detect new content instead of silently downloading or applying it. When an update is available, users can start it from the update card or About page and follow download, verification, and application progress.

details.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only 304 English onlyEnglish only
details.en: Re-published identical content is recognized as already current, while a temporary 304 response no longer clears the previously recorded update or error state.

sources.title: feat(update): English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
feat(update): English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only

title.en: Updates no longer damage the active version

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: New content is now fully extracted and verified in a temporary directory before atomically replacing the old one. Version directories still referenced by any update channel are protected, preserving the working installation when activation fails.

details.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyMiko English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
details.en: If a new train reuses the same directory name with identical content, Miko reuses the existing directory; conflicting content is rejected instead of overwriting it.

sources.title: fix(update): stop deleting the live version dir before the new one is ready

title.en: Displayed versions match the active content

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: The update card, About page, and post-update announcement now consistently show the active content version, avoiding mismatches between the application shell and the interface or server content in use.

sources.title: feat(update): English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Fixes the shell-version floor for component updates so new update trains no longer require the shell version to match the train version exactly, allowing already-installed clients to keep receiving component updates.

title.en: Update trains use a fixed shell compatibility floor

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: When publishing update trains, the minimum shell version is now a manually maintained compatibility floor instead of the train version itself, so each new train no longer blocks existing clients.

details.zh: English only 0.386.5English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
details.en: The current floor is 0.386.5, matching the live train; raise it only when train contents start depending on capabilities older shells cannot run.

sources.title: fix(release): use a fixed shell compatibility floor for train manifests

summary.zh: Miko English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only Windows English onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Miko now has a complete signed publishing pipeline for component updates, allowing installed clients to safely receive server and renderer updates. This release also fixes Windows installation checks and update-history dialog scrolling.

title.en: Signed component updates can now be published

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Each release now publishes server and renderer archives and produces signed, resumable update trains for stable and beta channels, so installed clients can safely discover and download component updates.

details.en: Interrupted publishing can resume from existing assets without duplicating or rolling back update sequence numbers.
Failures while reading remote release state now stop explicitly instead of being mistaken for a first publication.

sources.title: feat(release): add hot-update train publishing pipeline
fix(release): only treat gh's missing-release error as release-absent

title.en: Windows installation checks support the new component archives

summary.zh: Windows English only seed English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: The Windows installer and pre-launch checks now validate the new signed seed archives instead of incorrectly reporting corruption while looking for retired loose server files.

sources.title: fix(windows): install integrity checks use the seed archive layout

title.en: Update history scrolls correctly inside Settings

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: The update-history dialog now stays within the visible Settings panel and scrolls long release histories inside the dialog.

sources.title: fix(settings): constrain update history to settings panel

summary.zh: Miko English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Miko can now safely stage component updates in the background and apply them in place, with automatic rollback and one-click repair when needed. This release also brings a systematic cleanup to Settings, update history, and new-session composing.

title.en: Component updates stage quietly and apply in place

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Server and renderer components are verified and staged as one signed update, then applied with an in-place refresh instead of turning every component upgrade into a full reinstall.

details.en: An update becomes available only after both component sets are fully downloaded and verified.
Settings and the sidebar sticker share one update state, with stable and beta channels kept in sync.

sources.title: feat(runtime): ship signed artifact trains and unify settings

title.en: Automatic rollback and manual component repair

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyMiko English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: If new components repeatedly fail to start, Miko quarantines the bad version and returns to the previous working set. A repair action is also available from the tray and command line.

details.en: Both server and renderer startup are covered by crash counting and rollback protection.
Cleanup removes only old component versions that are no longer referenced by any update channel.

sources.title: feat(runtime): ship signed artifact trains and unify settings

title.en: A clearer update history across releases

summary.zh: “English only”English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: The About page now shows bilingual notes for the five newest published releases, while first-launch announcements collect every unread digest when an upgrade spans multiple versions.

details.en: If online history is unavailable, Miko clearly falls back to the offline history bundled with the app.

sources.title: feat(runtime): ship signed artifact trains and unify settings

title.en: GitHub update source

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only GitHubEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Component updates use GitHub for stable and beta selection across download, activation, and rollback.

sources.title: feat(runtime): ship signed artifact trains and unify settings

title.en: Settings now follows one consistent layout system

summary.zh: English only 884px English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: The Settings window now keeps a fixed 884px shell instead of changing width between tabs. Cards, rows, toggles, and sections share consistent primitives while preserving the existing visual style.

details.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyAgentEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
details.en: Browser, Interface, Agent, Media, and other tabs now use the same content track and spacing rules.
Labels, descriptions, and trailing actions now align consistently across controls of the same type.

sources.title: feat(runtime): ship signed artifact trains and unify settings

title.en: New-session drafts, skill badges, and send state stay in sync

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only Skill English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Welcome and pending sessions now have stable draft identities. After restoring a draft or inserting a skill badge, the editor, send button, and submit path use the same content decision.

details.zh: English only Skill English onlyEnglish only
details.en: Skill-badge-only messages are no longer silently treated as empty.
A failed send from a pending session now shows an explicit error instead of doing nothing.

sources.title: Pending session and composer consistency
feat(runtime): ship signed artifact trains and unify settings

title.en: Beta updates no longer delete newly activated components

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Component cleanup now protects versions referenced by every channel, fixing a beta-channel first-launch failure where newly activated components could be removed by mistake.

sources.title: feat(runtime): ship signed artifact trains and unify settings

title.en: Clearer first-run preparation

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only“English only”English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: While a fresh install extracts and verifies its components, a standalone splash screen keeps showing the preparation state without depending on renderer assets that are not ready yet.

sources.title: feat(runtime): ship signed artifact trains and unify settings

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Manual context compaction now targets sessions by ID and clearly reports accepted, noop, or failed outcomes.

title.en: Manual compaction is session-ID authoritative

summary.zh: English onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish onlyEnglish only
summary.en: Manual compaction now hits the focused session reliably and surfaces accepted, noop, or failure feedback instead of silent path-based guesses.

details.zh: English only ID English onlyEnglish only
details.en: Desktop and mobile manual compaction both resolve targets by session ID.
Legacy path addressing remains converted at the protocol boundary, with explicit terminal outcomes.

sources.title: fix: make manual compaction session-id authoritative
